Title: Refactor converter entrypoint into a builder surface
Description: Replace the hardcoded converter construction in `internal/convert/convert.go` with a builder-based control surface while preserving the public `ToMarkdown` entry point
Acceptance: `internal/convert/builder.go` exists, `ToMarkdown` delegates through the builder surface, and current parser behavior still passes existing convert and engine tests

Title: Add explicit hook registration points
Description: Introduce a dedicated hooks layer for converter customization so before/after processing logic can be added without rewriting the converter entry point
Acceptance: `internal/convert/hooks.go` exists, hook registration is explicit, and the default converter path remains deterministic

Title: Add explicit plugin registry and default converter chain
Description: Move the default plugin stack into a dedicated registry so `markmaton` owns its default converter composition instead of hardcoding it inline
Acceptance: `internal/convert/plugins.go` exists, the default plugin chain is explicit, and current default output still matches protected baselines

Title: Add converter-surface unit coverage
Description: Add focused Go tests for builder determinism, hook execution, and default plugin registration so future converter-level work has a tight safety net
Acceptance: `internal/convert` has targeted tests for builder construction, hook execution, and default plugin composition
Notes: Do not rely on engine tests alone

Title: Curate promoted structure-heavy fixture shortlist
Description: Choose the exact benchmark rows to promote into repo fixtures, with at least one discussion/Q&A fixture and one app-shell/timeline fixture, based on reusable parser patterns rather than site vanity
Acceptance: A short promotion decision is reflected in `docs/benchmark-matrix.md`, and the selected benchmark rows have explicit rationale tied to general parser structure
Test_Method: manual: review `docs/benchmark-matrix.md` and local benchmark cache, confirm the promoted rows represent reusable parser patterns
Notes: Promoted BM-13 and BM-11 for this epic

Title: Promote a discussion/Q&A regression fixture
Description: Copy the chosen discussion/Q&A sample from local benchmark cache into `testdata/fixtures/regression/` and wire it into the repo as a durable regression input
Acceptance: A promoted discussion/Q&A fixture exists under `testdata/fixtures/regression/` and captures thread or answer hierarchy that current synthetic fixtures do not cover
Test_Method: manual: inspect the promoted fixture and verify it matches the chosen benchmark row and parser pattern

Title: Promote an app-shell or timeline regression fixture
Description: Copy the chosen app-shell/timeline sample from local benchmark cache into `testdata/fixtures/regression/` so converter-level structure work has a realistic regression target
Acceptance: A promoted app-shell/timeline fixture exists under `testdata/fixtures/regression/` and captures structure that the current simplified repo fixture does not cover
Test_Method: manual: inspect the promoted fixture and verify it matches the chosen benchmark row and parser pattern

Title: Extend engine regression coverage for promoted fixtures
Description: Add engine-level regression assertions for the newly promoted structure-heavy fixtures while preserving guardrails for careers, job detail, and clean docs baselines
Acceptance: `internal/engine/process_test.go` covers the promoted fixtures and still protects existing strong page classes from regression
Notes: Keep assertions behavioral, not byte-for-byte for real pages

Title: Implement first discussion/Q&A converter customization
Description: Add the first general converter-level customization aimed at thread/Q&A structure so question and answer blocks read more clearly without site-name rules
Acceptance: The promoted discussion/Q&A fixture becomes materially more readable, the change lives in `internal/convert`, and no site-specific rule names are introduced
Notes: Implemented generic opening-shell trimming, control-line removal, and discussion-structure cleanup without site-name rules

Title: Implement first app-shell or timeline converter customization
Description: Add the first general converter-level customization for app-shell/timeline structure so issue/PR/repo-like pages have better block ordering and grouping without site-name rules
Acceptance: The promoted app-shell/timeline fixture improves materially, the change stays in the converter layer, and existing strong classes still hold
Notes: Implemented generic timeline opening cleanup, duplicate-status collapse, and redundant heading-echo removal

Title: Update benchmark documentation for converter-era promotion
Description: Reflect the promoted fixtures and new converter-layer focus in the benchmark docs so the next round of work stays grounded in page patterns and not site patching
Acceptance: `docs/benchmark-matrix.md` reflects the promoted rows, what moved into converter-layer work, and what remains outside this epic
Test_Method: manual: review `docs/benchmark-matrix.md` and confirm promoted fixtures and next-layer guidance are current

Title: Run full regression and wrapper contract checks
Description: After converter customization lands, run the full Go suite and the Python boundary suite to confirm that the repo-wide parser behavior and wrapper contract still hold
Acceptance: `go test ./...` passes and Python unit tests pass without changing the public request/response contract
Test_Method: env GOCACHE=/tmp/markmaton-go-build GOMODCACHE=/tmp/markmaton-go-mod go test ./... | PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/markmaton-pyc python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'
Notes: Final regression gate for the epic

Title: Validate converter-layer planning artifacts
Description: Validate that the new converter-layer plan and issue CSV are well-formed and aligned with the benchmark-driven next epic
Acceptance: The plan file exists, the CSV passes validation, and the issue ordering matches the converter-layer epic structure
